differential_diagnoses: Hypervirulent Klebsiella pneumoniae infection with endogenous endophthalmitis and liver abscess
Disseminated fungal infection (e.g., Candida or Aspergillus species)
Metastatic lung adenocarcinoma with paraneoplastic or infectious complications
Staphylococcus aureus sepsis with metastatic endophthalmitis
next_steps: Perform diagnostic and therapeutic aspiration of the hepatic abscess for gram stain and culture
Perform transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) to exclude endocarditis
Perform diagnostic vitreous tap of the right eye for microbial culture and PCR testing
Perform image-guided lung biopsy if initial cultures are non-diagnostic
Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotic coverage targeting hypervirulent Klebsiella (if not already optimized)
Consult ophthalmology for urgent evaluation and potential intraocular antibiotic injection
